ebe.replace.scope.hint:
Same Type: Replace all blocks of the same type as source
By Condition: Replace by property conditions (WIP)
Custom: Replace currently selected blocks

ebe.translate.hint: Select blocks first, then enter offsets to translate
ebe.layer.merge_into_hint: Choose the target layer. Regions and selected block ownership in this layer will move into the target layer, then the source layer will be removed.
ebe.replace.same_type.hint: Select source block type and target block, replace all same-type blocks
ebe.replace.condition.hint: Select source block type, target block and property condition, replace only matching blocks
ebe.replace.custom.hint: Select blocks in 3D viewport first, then choose target block to replace selected blocks
ebe.rotate.hint: Select blocks first, choose rotation axis and angle, then execute
ebe.fill.random.hint: Add multiple blocks with custom weights for random fill
ebe.mirror.hint: Mirror selected blocks along the chosen axis
ebe.printer.select_chest_hint: Left-click the chest/barrel to bind as material source
ebe.printer.select_chest_invalid: That block is not a valid container
ebe.editor.files.hot_reload_hint: Drop files into the schematics folder; this list hot-reloads.
ebe.materials.large_refresh_hint: Material counting is paused for this very large projection to keep the editor responsive. Click refresh in this panel when you need the full material list.
ebe.workgroup.unique_hint: Create a workgroup or join an existing one. A player can only belong to one workgroup at a time.

ebe.help.manual.title: Easy Block Editor User Manual
ebe.help.manual.search: Search features, entries, settings, or issues...
ebe.help.manual.ui_map.title: UI map: every area is understandable and operable
ebe.help.manual.ui_map.right: Right panel: properties, materials, history, NBT
ebe.help.manual.page.about.title: About the Mod
ebe.help.manual.page.about.summary: Easy Block Editor is an in-game toolkit for building editing, projection management, printing, and collaboration.
ebe.help.manual.page.about.body: ## Identity|Current version: v%s. Author signature: L1ghT.|Easy Block Editor centers on an in-game editor and connects the file library, 3D viewport, projections, material lists, printer, workgroups, and admin console.|It is useful for solo building cleanup and for server teams that split, sync, verify, and print large projections step by step.|! This manual will keep growing with the mod. The search box matches titles, categories, summaries, and body text; matching text on the opened page is shown with a blue selection background.
ebe.help.manual.page.quick_start.title: Five-Minute Start
ebe.help.manual.page.quick_start.summary: The shortest route from opening the editor to loading a projection, checking materials, and generating a projection.
ebe.help.manual.page.quick_start.body: ## Entry|Run /ebe open in game, or use the Architect Toolbox to open the main editor.|File > Open reads projects from the schematic folder. File > Import opens the native file picker so you can bring in external projection files.|## First Steps|After opening or importing .ebe, .litematic, .schem, .schematic, or .nbt, watch the status bar move from reading the file to preparing the viewport.|Once the viewport is ready, rotate the camera with the mouse and use the toolbar to select, place, delete, replace, grab, measure, and fill.|The material panel can refresh the material list. Very large projections pause automatic counting so the UI does not freeze while opening.|When you are ready to build on a server, generate a projection from the projection panel, then use the projection remote or printer workflow.
ebe.help.manual.page.ui_overview.title: Main UI Overview
ebe.help.manual.page.ui_overview.summary: Understand the top menu, side panels, 3D viewport, upper-right buttons, and floating toolbar.
ebe.help.manual.page.ui_overview.body: ## Top Area|The top-left menu contains File, Edit, View, Tool, Projection, and Help. The upper-right area keeps the workgroup button and the player client settings button.|The workgroup button sits to the left of settings and opens the collaboration panel inside the main UI. The settings button is still local client settings, not admin settings.|## Viewport|The 3D viewport is the building preview and editing area. Selection, grab, replace, fill, and display filters all operate on blocks shown here.|## Side Panels|The left panel mainly hosts files and layers. The right panel hosts properties, display, materials, history, and NBT. They keep frequent editing actions close to the viewport.|! The thin dividers beside the viewport can be dragged to resize the left and right panels. The cursor changes to a horizontal resize shape when resizing is available.
ebe.help.manual.page.layout_operations.s: How to resize panels, move the floating toolbar, use context actions, search boxes, and scroll areas.
ebe.help.manual.page.layout_operations.b: ## Panel Resize|The left and right panels have draggable borders on the side facing the viewport. Drag them horizontally to balance file management, inspection panels, and viewport space.|When your mouse is over a draggable border, the pointer changes to a horizontal resize cursor as a clear hint.|## Floating Toolbar|The floating toolbar can be dragged to a comfortable position and collapsed or expanded. For large projections, move it away from the building body.|## Scrolling and Search|File lists, materials, history, NBT, and manual pages use vertical scrollbars so content is not clipped.|Search fields update results in real time. In this manual, clicking a search result opens that page and highlights matching text with a blue selected-text background.|## Items and In-World Use|The Architect Toolbox opens the editor. The Projection Remote moves, rotates, and fine-tunes projections in the world. Key bindings can be reviewed and recorded in Settings > Keybindings.
ebe.help.manual.page.file_library.title: Files Page and Formats
ebe.help.manual.page.file_library.summar: The left file page supports projection browsing, hot reload, thumbnail details, and common file actions.
ebe.help.manual.page.file_library.body: ## Supported Formats|The file library recognizes .ebe, .litematic, .schem, .schematic, and .nbt. The list shows file type so you can identify the source quickly.|.ebe is the mod project format and is best for preserving editor state. .litematic, .schem, .schematic, and .nbt are for tool interoperability.|## Hot Reload|Drop files into the client schematic folder and the file page refreshes automatically. You do not need to close and reopen the editor.|## Context Actions|File rows support open, rename, delete, and export-as actions. Delete shows a confirmation first.|## Thumbnail Details|Rows show file type and size. Very large projections use more conservative loading to protect UI responsiveness.
ebe.help.manual.page.open_import.title: Open and Import
ebe.help.manual.page.open_import.summary: Open reads workspace files; Import loads external projections through the native file picker.
ebe.help.manual.page.open_import.body: ## Open|File > Open targets files already in the schematic folder and is best for continuing a project that is already in the workspace.|Clicking a file in the left file page is also an open action.|## Import|File > Import opens the Windows native file picker. Use it to choose .ebe, .litematic, .schem, .schematic, or .nbt from Desktop, Downloads, or another folder.|The distinction is: Import brings external files in, while Open reads files that already belong to the workspace.|## Loading Phases|The status bar reports reading, viewport preparation, and loading progress. Huge projections may allow camera motion before all chunks finish rendering; this avoids freezing the whole UI for too long.
ebe.help.manual.page.save_export.title: Save, Save As, and Export
ebe.help.manual.page.save_export.summary: Save keeps the current file format when possible; Save As and Export create a selected target format.
ebe.help.manual.page.save_export.body: ## Save|File > Save writes back the current project. For files with an existing source format, the editor tries to preserve the format semantics and avoid unnecessary conversion.|## Save As|File > Save As creates a new file name. Check the extension before saving if you need a specific target format.|## Export|File > Export is for external use, such as writing the current build as .schem, .schematic, .litematic, .nbt, or .ebe.|## Recommendation|Use .ebe while actively editing. Export to another format when handing the structure to another tool or server workflow.|! For large projects, save a manual version after important milestones. The history panel and version tags make experimental edits easier to roll back.
ebe.help.manual.page.editing_tools.title: Editing Tools
ebe.help.manual.page.editing_tools.summa: What select, place, delete, replace, grab, measure, fill, rotate, mirror, and translate are for.
ebe.help.manual.page.editing_tools.body: ## Common Tools|Select marks blocks or regions. Place uses the current block. Delete removes target blocks. Replace changes blocks that match the configured condition.|Grab samples a block state from the viewport and is useful for copying complex properties. Measure checks dimensions. Fill quickly fills a selection or condition range.|## Transforms|Rotate, mirror, and translate are useful for changing selection or structure direction. Confirm selection and center point before applying them.|## History|Undo, redo, tags, and branches are managed in the history panel. Create a tag before complex edits so experiments remain recoverable.|! Replace and fill support finer conditions. When NBT is involved, decide whether NBT should be matched; otherwise visually similar but data-different blocks may be handled together.
ebe.help.manual.page.selection_display.s: Use selections, same-type selection, row/column/layer display, and NBT filters for complex structures.
ebe.help.manual.page.selection_display.b: ## Selection Modes|You can select all, deselect, point-select, multi-select, box-select, and select blocks of the same type. Different modes fit different editing phases.|## Display Filters|The display panel can show all blocks, a block type, a Y layer, rows or columns, or the current selection. It does not delete blocks; it only changes visibility.|## NBT Filter|When NBT-sensitive matching is enabled, block state and block entity data are both considered. Chests, beds, signs, and other data-bearing blocks need special attention.|## Practical Uses|For huge projections, inspect a layer or selection first. For material checks, filter by block type. For containers or decorative entities, open the NBT panel and verify their data.
ebe.help.manual.page.materials.title: Material List
ebe.help.manual.page.materials.summary: Count required blocks and compare them with inventory or nearby chest materials.
ebe.help.manual.page.materials.body: ## Refresh Materials|The right material panel refreshes required block counts for the current model. Huge projections do not force automatic refresh, avoiding UI stalls while opening files.|## Inventory Compare|The material panel can compare against the player inventory and quickly show missing blocks.|## Printer Material Sources|The printer can use materials from the player inventory and nearby chests. Check the source range in settings before building.|## Export and Diff|Material lists and diff results are useful for team preparation. In workgroups, shared material communication reduces duplicate gathering.
ebe.help.manual.page.projection.title: Projection Operations
ebe.help.manual.page.projection.summary: Generate, unload, reposition, rotate, mirror, track completion, and use the projection remote.
ebe.help.manual.page.projection.body: ## Generate and Unload|The projection menu or panel can generate a projection from the current build and unload it later. The generated projection appears as a translucent structure in the world.|## Placement|Place at player feet or enter custom coordinates. Center point settings affect rotation and alignment.|## Adjustment|The projection panel supports position, rotation, mirror, and center adjustments. Holding Shift usually uses larger steps.|## Remote|The Projection Remote fine-tunes position and facing in the world. Movement, vertical adjustment, and rotation bindings are shown in item tooltips and keybinding settings.|## Completion|Projection progress records placed blocks and completion percentage. In multiplayer collaboration, synced progress helps prevent duplicate work.
ebe.help.manual.page.printer.title: Printer and Place All
ebe.help.manual.page.printer.summary: Manual printing, auto printing, parallel lanes, material sources, and server permissions.
ebe.help.manual.page.printer.body: ## Printer Modes|Manual printing is cautious. Auto printing is for material-ready situations where the server allows it.|## Parallel Lanes|More parallel lanes make one player attempt placement more aggressively. Server performance, client performance, latency, and permission checks all affect real speed.|## Materials|The printer can draw from inventory and nearby chests. If materials are missing, refresh the material list and verify source chests.|## Place All|Place All is a high-impact operation and may be restricted by server permissions. Check server rules before using it publicly.|! During fast multiplayer printing, the server must treat the real world state as authoritative. Workgroup sync and placement checks reduce the chance that multiple players handle the same block.
ebe.help.manual.page.workgroups.title: Workgroup Collaboration
ebe.help.manual.page.workgroups.summary: Create, join, leave, disband, kick members, and sync projections with a team.
ebe.help.manual.page.workgroups.body: ## Entry|The workgroup button in the upper-right of the main UI opens the workgroup panel. It is to the left of the player settings button.|## Unique Membership|A player can belong to only one workgroup at a time. This prevents multiple projection targets from causing sync, render, or printer confusion.|## Member Actions|Members can join and leave. Leaders can manage members, kick members, edit collaboration information, and disband the group. A leader cannot simply leave; they must disband or transfer leadership first.|## Projection Sync|Workgroups sync active projections so members see the same collaboration targets. When a group is disbanded, all members are forced out and the data is cleared.|## Empty Cleanup|If an empty workgroup appears, the server automatically disbands and cleans it up to avoid stale memory use.
ebe.help.manual.page.admin.title: Admin Panel and Permissions
ebe.help.manual.page.admin.summary: Use /ebe admin to open the standalone admin UI for permissions, server settings, and workgroups.
ebe.help.manual.page.admin.body: ## Opening|Run /ebe admin to open the standalone admin panel. It is not inside the main editor UI because it controls server-wide behavior.|## Permissions|Admins can set global permissions for editor, projection, printer, place all, collaboration, file library, import, and export, and can add per-player overrides.|## Server Settings|Server global settings are persistent. In-game changes are saved and hot-reloaded immediately.|## Workgroup Management|Admins can view server workgroups, disband groups, or kick specific members. Leaders cannot be kicked; disband the group instead.|! If a player reports disabled actions, first check admin permissions, then confirm that the server has EBE installed and server features enabled.
ebe.help.manual.page.client_settings.sum: The upper-right settings button is client-side and controls editor experience, projections, printer, and keybindings.
ebe.help.manual.page.client_settings.bod: ## Entry|The gear button in the upper-right of the main UI is player client settings. It must remain available and is not the same as /ebe admin.|## General|Set the schematic folder and theme. The folder controls what the left file page reads.|## Editor|Set FOV, flight speed, history limit, and viewport shader strategy.|## Projection and Printer|Projection opacity, projection render distance, printer range, material source range, and printer parallelism are adjusted here.|## Keybindings|The keybindings page can record and reset keys. Press the desired key while recording to finish the binding.
Recommended settings for huge projection loading, viewport render distance, moving degradation, FBO scaling, and loading budgets.

## Huge Projections|Projections above 3 MB put heavy pressure on reading, computation, and viewport chunk building. The editor prepares the viewport in batches so you can move the camera sooner.|## Render Distance|Viewport render distance 0 means unlimited and tries to show the whole projection. A positive value only shows nearby regions, reducing stutter but hiding distant parts.|## Moving Degradation|Degrade while moving reduces build and draw pressure during camera motion. After you stop moving, the viewport gradually restores quality.|## FBO Scaling|Dynamic FBO scaling lowers viewport resolution while moving, trading a softer image for smoother camera control.|## Budgets|Higher compile budget, load budget, and blocks-per-frame complete faster, but can make the UI stutter. Performance mode is for huge projections; quality mode is for screenshots and careful polishing.|! Stronger CPU and GPU help loading and camera motion, but rendering still has to submit work on Minecraft's render thread. Not every bottleneck can be moved to a background thread.
ebe.help.manual.page.shortcuts.title: Shortcuts and Input Focus
ebe.help.manual.page.shortcuts.summary: Notes about keybindings, focused text fields, and projection remote keys.
ebe.help.manual.page.shortcuts.body: ## Keybinding Page|Settings > Keybindings lists edit actions, tool switching, flight, mouse operations, and projection remote categories.|## Text Field Focus|When a search box, number input, or name input is focused, the editor avoids treating typed keys as viewport shortcuts.|## Recommended Habit|Bind high-frequency tools comfortably: select, place, delete, replace, grab, undo, and redo.|## Remote|Projection remote movement and rotation should be distinct from flight keys so adjusting a projection does not accidentally move the player or camera.
ebe.help.manual.page.nbt_advanced.title: NBT, Templates, and Field Diff
ebe.help.manual.page.nbt_advanced.summar: Use the NBT panel, templates, and field-level diff for containers, beds, signs, and data-bearing blocks.
ebe.help.manual.page.nbt_advanced.body: ## NBT Panel|After selecting a block with block entity data, inspect and edit fields in the NBT panel.|## NBT Templates|Save the current NBT as a template and apply it to similar blocks. This is useful for container names, sign text, and decorative data.|## Field-Level Diff|Template diff compares fields directly, showing exactly which field changed instead of only reporting that the entire NBT differs.|## Decorative Entities|Structures with decorative entities or special data need careful save/load testing. Some target formats cannot represent every piece of data.|! Save a version tag before changing NBT. Bulk application can be undone, but verification becomes more expensive.
ebe.help.manual.page.troubleshooting.sum: How to investigate slow loading, invisible blocks, permission failures, missing materials, and incompatible formats.
ebe.help.manual.page.troubleshooting.bod: ## Huge Projection Is Slow|Switch to performance mode, enable moving degradation and dynamic FBO scaling, and lower per-frame budget peaks. Return to balanced or quality after loading.|## Viewport Shows Only a Strip|Check viewport render distance. Distance 0 tries to show the whole projection; a positive value clips distant areas.|## Chests and Beds Show but Blocks Look Wrong|This is usually related to viewport render distance, chunk build progress, or display filters. Reset filters first, then try render distance 0.|## Permission Failure|Confirm that the server has EBE installed and that the admin panel allows the player to use the feature.|## Missing Materials|Refresh the material list and check inventory plus material-source chest range. Chests outside the configured range are ignored by the printer.|## Format Issues|If an external file fails to import, confirm that the extension matches the real content. Some old .schematic files have incomplete data and may need conversion in another tool first.
